name: Snow Caps
recipeIngredient: 2 White chocolate baking bars, chopped (2 oz each; optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200°F.
2. Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
3. In a large mixer bowl, combine egg whites and cream of tartar. Beat at the highest speed of an electric mixer until the mixture is just frothy.
4. Add sugar, one tablespoon at a time, beating well after each addition. Continue beating until stiff peaks form.
5. Add vanilla; beat for one minute.
6. Fold in chocolate chips.
7. Drop mixture by teaspoonfuls (1 inch apart) onto prepared baking sheets.
8. Bake for 2 hours or until meringues are thoroughly dry to the touch but not browned, rotating baking sheets halfway through baking.
9. Turn off heat. Leave in closed oven for 3-4 hours or until completely dry.
10. Remove from oven and cool completely.
11. Carefully remove from parchment.
12. Melt white chocolate in the top of a double boiler over hot, not boiling, water. Stir constantly until chocolate melts.
13. Dip the top of each cookie into melted chocolate, if desired. Place on waxed paper to dry.
14. Store at room temperature in tightly covered containers.
Makes about 6 dozen cookies.
